message:
perf(vfs,wal): shard inode lock table and fix WAL FEC receiver mutability

unix.rs -- InodeTable sharding:
- Replace the single-mutex global InodeTable with a 16-shard array of
  independently locked HashMaps. The shard index is derived from a
  DefaultHasher hash of the InodeKey, masked to the shard count (power
  of two for branchless modulus). This reduces lock contention on
  multi-threaded workloads where many connections open distinct database
  files, since concurrent get/get_or_create/maybe_remove calls on
  different inodes will typically hit different shards.
- get(), get_or_create(), and maybe_remove() all route through
  shard_idx() to select the correct shard before acquiring its mutex.

wal_fec.rs -- receiver mutability fix:
- Change drain_abandoned_work() parameter from &mpsc::Receiver to
  &mut mpsc::Receiver, and update all four call sites in
  run_repair_pipeline_worker() accordingly. This resolves a borrow
  checker issue where try_recv() requires exclusive access to the
  receiver on newer channel implementations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
